days.title: Asking and telling the hour (часът е…)

grammar.title: Asking the time and saying the full hour
grammar.explanation: To ask the time in Bulgarian you say 'Колко е часът?' (literally 'How much is the hour?'). Note that часът carries the definite article suffix -ът, because you are asking about THE hour. To answer, use the present-tense verb 'to be' (е for 'it is') plus the number: 'Часът е два.' (It is two o'clock) or simply 'Два часа.' After numbers 2-12 the word for hour is часа (a special counting form), while with едно you say 'един час' but most naturally just 'един'. You can add точно (exactly) for emphasis: 'Часът е точно пет.' Remember Bulgarian has no cases — the number just sits before часа with no special ending.

examples.english: What time is it?
It is five o'clock.
It is exactly nine o'clock now.

reading.title: Колко е часът?
reading.text: — Колко е часът сега? — Часът е точно три. — Благодаря, имам само една минута!
reading.translation: — What time is it now? — It is exactly three o'clock. — Thank you, I have only one minute!

days.speakingPrompt: Imagine a stranger stops you on the street. Ask them 'Колко е часът?' and then give a full-hour answer of your own (e.g. 'Часът е седем.'). Say both lines aloud.

days.title: Half past and quarters (и половина, и четвърт)

vocab.exampleEn: It is a quarter past three.
It is a quarter to six.

vocab.example: Имам среща в два и половина.
vocab.exampleEn: I have a meeting at half past two.
I am late for the meeting.

grammar.title: Minutes past and minutes to the hour
grammar.explanation: For minutes after the hour, Bulgarian uses 'и' (and): 'Часът е пет и десет' = ten past five (literally 'five and ten'). For half past, use 'и половина': 'два и половина' = half past two. For a quarter past, use 'и четвърт'. For minutes before the next hour, use 'без' (without): 'шест без десет' = ten to six, and 'без четвърт' = a quarter to. Notice 'без' literally means 'without', so you name the coming hour minus the minutes. The verb 'to be' (е) still introduces the time: 'Часът е девет без петнайсет.'

examples.english: It is half past four.
It is a quarter past seven.
It is a quarter to ten.

reading.title: Срещата
reading.text: — Имам среща в два и половина. — Сега е два без четвърт. — Добре, не закъснявам.
reading.translation: — I have a meeting at half past two. — It is a quarter to two now. — Good, I am not late.

days.speakingPrompt: Describe your day using three different times: one full hour, one 'и половина', and one 'без четвърт'. For example: 'Закуската е в осем. Срещата е в десет и половина. Обядът е в един без четвърт.'

days.title: Parts of the day (сутрин, вечер)

vocab.exampleEn: In the morning I drink coffee.
In the afternoon I work at home.
In the evening I watch television.
I am having a nice day.
I get up early in the morning.

grammar.title: Naming the part of the day
grammar.explanation: Bulgarian uses the same words as both nouns and time adverbs. 'Сутрин' means both 'morning' and 'in the morning'. You can simply place them before the verb: 'Сутрин пия чай' = I drink tea in the morning. To say 'during the night/day' use 'през' + the definite form: 'през нощта' (during the night), 'през деня' (during the day). With clock times you add the part of day for clarity: 'Часът е седем сутринта' (seven in the morning) versus 'седем вечерта' (seven in the evening). Notice сутринта and вечерта take the feminine -та article suffix.

examples.english: In the morning I get up early.
It is eight in the evening.
During the night it is quiet.

reading.title: Моят ден
reading.text: Сутрин ставам рано и пия кафе. Следобед работя. Вечер гледам телевизия, но не си лягам късно.
reading.translation: In the morning I get up early and drink coffee. In the afternoon I work. In the evening I watch television, but I don't go to bed late.

days.speakingPrompt: Say what you do сутрин, следобед and вечер. Use at least one clock time with a part of day, e.g. 'Часът е девет сутринта и пия кафе.'

days.title: Days of the week (дните на седмицата)

vocab.exampleEn: On Tuesday I have a meeting.
On Friday I go out with friends.
On Sunday I sleep in late.

grammar.title: Saying 'on' a day of the week
grammar.explanation: To say something happens on a given day, use the preposition 'в' + the day, with no special ending (Bulgarian has no cases): 'в понеделник' (on Monday), 'в петък' (on Friday). When the next word starts with в or ф, 'в' becomes 'във' for easy pronunciation: 'във вторник'. Days are not capitalised in Bulgarian. To say 'every Monday' you can use the plural with article: 'в понеделниците' or simply 'всеки понеделник' (every Monday). The week starts on понеделник (Monday) in Bulgaria.

examples.english: On Wednesday I have a lesson.
On Tuesday I am busy.
Every Friday I go out.

reading.title: Седмицата ми
reading.text: В понеделник и сряда работя. Във вторник уча български. В събота и неделя почивам.
reading.translation: On Monday and Wednesday I work. On Tuesday I study Bulgarian. On Saturday and Sunday I rest.

days.speakingPrompt: Go through the week and say one thing you do each day, using 'в' (or 'във'): 'В понеделник…, във вторник…, в сряда…'. Try to name all seven days.

days.title: Months and seasons (месеците и сезоните)

grammar.title: Saying 'in' a month and naming seasons
grammar.explanation: To say something happens in a month, use 'през' + the month: 'през януари' (in January), 'през септември' (in September). Months, like days, are not capitalised in Bulgarian. Seasons also use 'през' with the definite form: 'през лятото' (in the summer), 'през зимата' (in the winter), 'през пролетта' (in the spring), 'през есента' (in the autumn). To say 'this month' use 'този месец' and 'next month' 'следващия месец'. Remember лято is neuter, so it takes the -то article: лятото.

examples.english: In June I travel.
In the summer it is warm.
This month is April.

reading.title: Сезоните
reading.text: Имаме четири сезона. През лятото е топло, а през зимата е студено. Обичам юни най-много.
reading.translation: We have four seasons. In the summer it is warm, and in the winter it is cold. I like June the most.

days.speakingPrompt: Name your favourite месец and сезон and say why, using 'през': 'Обичам юли, защото през лятото е топло.'

days.title: Dates and putting it together (коя дата е днес?)

vocab.example: Рожденият ми ден е през май.

grammar.title: Asking the date and combining time words
grammar.explanation: To ask the date say 'Коя дата е днес?' (What date is it today?) or 'Кой ден е днес?' (What day is it today?). To answer, name the day or use the number plus month: 'Днес е първи юни' (Today is the first of June) — the ordinal 'първи' agrees as masculine because ден/месец are masculine. Use 'днес' (today), 'утре' (tomorrow), 'вчера' (yesterday). With the past you need 'беше' (was): 'Вчера беше неделя.' Now you can combine everything: 'Днес е петък, часът е три следобед.' Remember there are no cases — prepositions like в, на, през do all the work.

examples.english: What is the date today?
Today is the first of June.
Tomorrow is a holiday.

reading.title: Какъв ден е днес?
reading.text: — Коя дата е днес? — Днес е първи юни, петък. — А утре е събота, празник!
reading.translation: — What is the date today? — Today is the first of June, Friday. — And tomorrow is Saturday, a holiday!

days.speakingPrompt: Say today's date, day and time together, then say what tomorrow and yesterday are: 'Днес е…, часът е…. Утре е…, вчера беше….'

days.title: Review: Time & calendar

grammar.title: Recap: telling time, days and months
grammar.explanation: This week you learned to tell the time and talk about the calendar. Ask the time with 'Колко е часът?' and answer with 'Часът е…' plus a number; add 'и половина', 'и четвърт' for past the hour and 'без' for before it. Mark the part of day with сутрин, следобед, вечер. Use 'в' (or 'във' before в/ф) for days of the week — 'в петък' — and 'през' for months and seasons — 'през юни', 'през лятото'. Ask the date with 'Коя дата е днес?' Throughout, the verb 'to be' (съм, си, е, сме, сте, са) links the subject to the time, and there are NO noun cases — only prepositions and word order.

examples.russian: Колко е часът? Часът е пет и половина.
examples.english: What time is it? It is half past five.

examples.russian: В понеделник, през юни, имам среща.
examples.english: On Monday, in June, I have a meeting.

examples.russian: Коя дата е днес? Днес е първи юни.
examples.english: What is the date today? Today is the first of June.

reading.title: Среща в петък
reading.text: — Колко е часът? — Три без четвърт. — Имаме среща в петък, през юни. — Добре, утре е петък!
reading.translation: — What time is it? — A quarter to three. — We have a meeting on Friday, in June. — Good, tomorrow is Friday!

days.speakingPrompt: Have a short conversation with yourself: ask and tell the time, say what day and month it is today, and arrange a meeting for another day. Use 'часът е…', a day with 'в', and a month with 'през'.
